turns.speaker: Hannah
turns.text: Good afternoon, Fernbank Dental Practice, Hannah speaking. How can I help?

turns.speaker: David
turns.text: Hello. I've just moved into the area and I'd like to register as a new patient, and book a check-up if that's possible.

turns.speaker: Hannah
turns.text: Of course, I can set that up for you now. Let me take a few details first. Could I have your surname, please?

turns.speaker: David
turns.text: Yes, it's Kearney.

turns.speaker: Hannah
turns.text: Could you spell that for me?

turns.speaker: David
turns.text: Certainly. It's K-E-A-R-N-E-Y. Kearney. And my first name is David.

turns.speaker: Hannah
turns.text: Thank you, David. And your home address?

turns.speaker: David
turns.text: It's seventeen Maple Avenue — sorry, no, let me correct that, it's Maple Crescent. Seventeen Maple Crescent.

turns.speaker: Hannah
turns.text: No problem — seventeen Maple Crescent. And a contact number?

turns.speaker: David
turns.text: My mobile is oh-seven-seven-double-oh, nine-double-oh, eight-one-two. So oh-seven-seven-oh-oh, nine-oh-oh, eight-one-two.

turns.speaker: Hannah
turns.text: Let me read that back — oh-seven-seven-oh-oh, nine-oh-oh, eight-one-two. Perfect. Now, are you registering for NHS or private treatment?

turns.speaker: David
turns.text: I'd assumed private, but actually, if there's an NHS place available, I'd prefer that.

turns.speaker: Hannah
turns.text: You're in luck — we do have NHS spaces at the moment, so I'll put you down for that. Now, for your first appointment we book what we call a new-patient examination. It's a bit longer than a routine check-up because the dentist does a full assessment. That's forty-five minutes, and the fee is twenty-five pounds thirty.

turns.speaker: David
turns.text: That's fine. When's the earliest you could fit me in?

turns.speaker: Hannah
turns.text: Let me look. We could do next Tuesday the fourteenth in the morning — oh, wait, no, the dentist is on a training course that day. Let me correct that. The first free slot is Thursday the sixteenth.

turns.speaker: David
turns.text: Thursday the sixteenth suits me. What time?

turns.speaker: Hannah
turns.text: I can offer you nine fifteen or half past ten. Which would you prefer?

turns.speaker: David
turns.text: Half past ten, please. Nine fifteen's a little early for me.

turns.speaker: Hannah
turns.text: Half past ten it is. One thing to remember — please bring a list of any medication you're currently taking, as the dentist will need it for your records. You don't need to bring anything else for the first visit.

turns.speaker: David
turns.text: Will do. And is there anything I should know about payment?

turns.speaker: Hannah
turns.text: You can pay by card on the day. And do try to arrive about ten minutes early to fill in a medical history form. If you ever need to cancel, we ask for at least twenty-four hours' notice, otherwise there's a small charge.

turns.speaker: David
turns.text: Understood. Thanks very much for your help, Hannah.

turns.speaker: Hannah
turns.text: You're very welcome. We'll see you on the sixteenth. Take care.

questionGroups.instructions_html: Which type of treatment does David register for?

questions.prompt_text: The fee for the first examination is £______.
To the appointment, David must bring a list of his ______.
Cancellations require at least ______ hours' notice.
